raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/year>, I made a {$470.00} purchase using my American Express card with XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX for two items : a Home Gym ( {$390.00} ) and a Storage Bag ( {$79.00} ). 

On XX/XX/year>, I received only the Storage Bag and an unsolicited accessory item. I did not receive the Home Gym. 
The merchant later admitted in writing ( XX/XX/year> ) that the Home Gym had not been delivered and was still awaiting release. Despite this, the merchant claimed to American Express that the order was properly fulfilled because one item was delivered and the other was back-ordered. 

I attempted to return the delivered items within the merchants stated return period, but the merchant repeatedly failed to provide return instructions. 

I submitted a billing dispute with American Express and uploaded detailed documentation, including the merchants written admission of non-delivery. American Express denied the dispute approximately 30 minutes after I submitted this new evidence, making it impossible that the materials were appropriately reviewed. 

American Express appears to have relied solely on the merchants assertion that the undelivered item was back-ordered, allowing the merchant to retain full payment despite non-delivery and refusal to process a return. I am requesting a good-faith review of this dispute and an explanation of how the evidence was evaluated.
